textPlain: Isn't awesome how the free hand of the market starts fixing our broken food industry only after a significant portion of the country gets out from under its boot? Just love the US of A more every day.

It's a surprising correlation. I'd expect that increasing popularity of such drug would lead to an increase in amount of sugar added because a) that would make them more "tasty" b) people would become more reckless aka "I can eat more because the pill will help me avoid weight gain". (edited for clarity)
It's just insane that I can't find biscuits will less sugar.Not aspartame or similar, just biscuits will half or one third of the sugar.
I know ~nothing about GLP-1 medsCan anyone ELI5 why they're having this effect?  Do they reduce impulsivity or make the existing foods no longer palatable?
Reminds me of the arms race between web ads and ad blockers.
Bring the high protein, high fiber foods

I’m not an expert, but GLP-1 is a hormone.Wouldn’t using something like this trigger anti-doping concerns if an athlete took it?In sports, manipulating appetite or insulin pathways sets off red flags immediately.It’s interesting to see the food industry treat the same biological mechanism mainly as a market trend rather than a medical one.

Pharma tail wagging the food dog.
It’s a sad fact that GLPs are changing food costs. Humans are strange animals.
